status: exact polar heat-collision cascade and unbounded-degree escape theorem
proof_boundary: This artifact proves local heat-jet identities, classifies an infinite polar multiplicity cascade, and shows that a non-exponential-polynomial Laguerre-Polya boundary can only be lost through unbounded Jensen degree. It does not rule out that escape, prove endpoint Laguerre-Polya membership, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

exact.multiple_root_recurrence: If mult_xi(J_d)>=m and Q=J_(d+1), then Q^(ell)(xi)=(d+1)_ell*Q(xi)/xi^ell for 0<=ell<=m.
exact.double_root_criterion: At a double root xi, forward real splitting requires Q(xi)*J_d''(xi)/xi<=0.
exact.higher_multiplicity_gate: For m>=3, a differentiable one-sided hyperbolic continuation requires Q(xi)=0.
exact.polar_multiplicity_rule: If Q is negative-root hyperbolic and J_d is its polar descent, mult_xi(J_d)=m>=2 implies mult_xi(Q)=m+1.
exact.cascade: If every J_D, D>=d, is hyperbolic and mult_xi(J_d)=m>=2, then mult_xi(J_D)=D-(d-m).
exact.classification: The cascade forces F(z)=exp(-z/xi)*R(z) with deg(R)<=d-m; conversely this form gives multiplicity at least D-(d-m), with equality when deg(R)=d-m.
exact.strictness: If F is Laguerre-Polya with positive coefficients and is not exponential-polynomial, every finite Jensen polynomial is strictly hyperbolic.
exact.degree_escape: At a non-exponential-polynomial LP boundary, the least nonhyperbolic Jensen degree on the bad side tends to infinity as lambda approaches the boundary.

rows.claim: Adjacent Jensen degrees are related simultaneously by a polar map and by the coefficient heat hierarchy.
A multiple root of the lower polar fixes the complete adjacent-degree Taylor jet through that multiplicity.
rows.formula: If mult_xi(J_d)>=m and Q=J_(d+1), then Q^(ell)(xi)=(d+1)_ell*Q(xi)/xi^ell for 0<=ell<=m.
rows.proof_boundary: Local algebra at one nonzero multiple root.

rows.claim: Every low heat derivative at a multiple root collapses to one adjacent-degree value with a shift-positive prefactor.
The adjacent Jensen value gives the complete first-order inward criterion at a nondegenerate double root.
rows.formula: At a double root xi, forward real splitting requires Q(xi)*J_d''(xi)/xi<=0.
rows.proof_boundary: Local double-root splitting, not global invariance.

rows.claim: A triple-or-higher collision cannot have a nonzero constant heat perturbation along a differentiable hyperbolic path.
rows.formula: For m>=3, a differentiable one-sided hyperbolic continuation requires Q(xi)=0.

rows.claim: A multiple lower-degree polar root lifts by exactly one multiplicity whenever the adjacent extension is hyperbolic.
rows.formula: If Q is negative-root hyperbolic and J_d is its polar descent, mult_xi(J_d)=m>=2 implies mult_xi(Q)=m+1.

rows.claim: If the whole upper Jensen tower is hyperbolic, one finite multiple root forces a linearly growing common-root multiplicity.
rows.formula: If every J_D, D>=d, is hyperbolic and mult_xi(J_d)=m>=2, then mult_xi(J_D)=D-(d-m).
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional on hyperbolicity of every higher degree.

rows.claim: The only entire coefficient families that can support that infinite fixed-root cascade are exponential times a bounded-degree polynomial.
rows.formula: The cascade forces F(z)=exp(-z/xi)*R(z) with deg(R)<=d-m; conversely this form gives multiplicity at least D-(d-m), with equality when deg(R)=d-m.
rows.proof_boundary: Classification of the cascade, not LP membership for zeta.

rows.claim: For a non-exponential-polynomial LP endpoint, every fixed Jensen degree is strict and any loss of hyperbolicity nearby must escape to unbounded degree.
rows.formula: If F is Laguerre-Polya with positive coefficients and is not exponential-polynomial, every finite Jensen polynomial is strictly hyperbolic. At a non-exponential-polynomial LP boundary, the least nonhyperbolic Jensen degree on the bad side tends to infinity as lambda approaches the boundary.
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional boundary geometry; it does not exclude the boundary.

rows.claim: A successful heat-flow proof must control the degree-rescaled collision layer rather than search for a first contact in a fixed degree.
rows.formula: derive a degree-uniform scaled-root or polar-defect barrier as D->infinity
rows.proof_boundary: Open all-degree estimate; not PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
